method.managed_cold: Five sequential AgentCore CLI invocations, each using a fresh Runtime session ID.
method.managed_warm: A second invocation in each of the five managed sessions.
method.local_cold: Five direct local Python invocations, constructing a fresh Strands Agent for each pair.
method.local_warm: A second invocation on each of the five local Agent instances.
prompt_pair: Fresh Seattle weather request with an explicit tool-use instruction.
Fresh Boston weather request with an explicit second tool-use instruction.
method.timing_boundary: Local timings cover Agent invocation. Managed end-to-end timings cover the AgentCore CLI call through receipt of the final response. Managed trace timings cover the Runtime POST /invocations span and its child spans.

claim_limits: N=5 per lane is a learning benchmark, not a performance guarantee.
Measurements were sequential and represent one account, Region, model, tool, prompt pair, and time window.
Managed CLI timings include client startup, authentication, network transit, and any Runtime session startup outside the traced application span.
The difference between CLI end-to-end time and the Runtime root span is unattributed overhead, not a direct microVM cold-start measurement.
Tool-span duration represents the instrumented weather tool call observed in these traces; it does not establish general OpenWeather latency.
